response.text:
# REFLECTION

r2c4 is in critical distress: SoC at 44.5% (14.37/37 kWh) while islanded with 1 kW continuous load, yielding ~14 hours to depletion. The microgrid is under systemic stress—r2c3 is at 28% SoC in survival mode, and all peers (r1c4, r2c5, r3c4) are making urgent/normal requests while simultaneously rejecting outbound transfers from r2c4. The pattern is clear: peers view r2c4's 44.5% as above their survival thresholds (50–60%), so they refuse to share; yet r2c4 cannot afford to give away energy without accelerating its own collapse. No trust circles exist, leaving r2c4 isolated. The geographic round-robin strategy has failed—requests are being rejected on the grounds that r2c4 must preserve its own margin. The only viable path forward is to flip the stance: stop attempting to share (which is being refused anyway) and instead issue urgent inbound requests, while minimizing any outbound commitment to preserve the critical 14.37 kWh buffer for as long as possible.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: [r2c3]
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded microgrid systemic stress; r2c4 SoC critical (44.5%); all peers rejecting transfers citing survival margins; r2c3 at 28% in asymmetric protection mode; geographic round-robin ineffective; must prioritize own resilience, suspend outbound sharing, and request inbound energy urgently to extend autonomy window."
ttl_ticks: 4
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
